meaning: 본질 — the most important quality or part of something
examples: The essence of studying a language is communication.
In essence, the two plans are the same.

meaning: 고려하다 — to think carefully about something
examples: Please consider re-rendering for optimization.
Have you considered learning with flashcards?

meaning: 최적화 — making something as good or efficient as possible
examples: Re-render optimization keeps the app fast.
The team focused on performance optimization.

meaning: 시행착오 — trying different things until one works
examples: I learned to code through trial and error.
We found the answer by trial and error.

meaning: 다른 말로 바꿔 표현하다 — to say the same thing in different words
examples: Can you paraphrase that sentence?
Paraphrasing helps you sound more natural.

meaning: 유창한 — able to speak a language smoothly and easily
examples: She is fluent in three languages.
I want to become fluent in English.

meaning: 어색한, 불편한 — causing or feeling embarrassment or discomfort
examples: There was an awkward silence in the meeting.
I felt awkward speaking in front of everyone.

meaning: ~와 잘 지내다 — to have a good relationship with someone
examples: I get along well with my coworkers.
Do you get along with your neighbors?

meaning: ~을 생각해내다 — to produce an idea or plan
examples: She came up with a great idea.
Let's come up with a plan together.

meaning: 결국, 마침내 — in the end, after a long time
examples: If you practice every day, you'll eventually become fluent.
We were lost, but we eventually found the way.

meaning: 관점 — a particular way of thinking about something
examples: From my perspective, speaking every day is key.
Try to see it from his perspective.

meaning: 주장하다, 논쟁하다 — to give reasons for or against something (not always a fight!)
examples: I would argue that speaking is more important than grammar.
They argued about the best study method.

meaning: 합리적인 — fair and sensible
examples: That sounds reasonable to me.
Is 30 minutes a day a reasonable goal?

meaning: 설득하다, 납득시키다 — to make someone believe or agree
examples: I couldn't convince him to try the new method.
Are you convinced that daily practice works?

meaning: ~하는 경향이 있다 — to usually do or be something
examples: I tend to forget new words if I don't review them.
Beginners tend to be afraid of making mistakes.

meaning: 명확히 하다 — to make something clearer or easier to understand
examples: Could you clarify what you mean?
Let me clarify my point — I wasn't being clear.

meaning: 다시 표현하다 — to say the same thing in different or clearer words
examples: Let me rephrase that — I wasn't very clear.
Can you rephrase your question?

meaning: 망설이다, 주저하다 — to pause before doing or saying something
examples: Don't hesitate to ask if you have a question.
I hesitated before speaking because I wasn't sure of the word.

meaning: 말이 막히다, 더듬다 — to have difficulty speaking smoothly
examples: I always stumble over long words.
It's okay to stumble — just keep going.

meaning: 자연스러운 — easy, relaxed, not forced or stiff
examples: Self-correction is a natural part of conversation.
Her English sounds very natural.

meaning: 일상, 루틴 — a fixed set of actions done regularly
examples: I built a morning routine that includes 20 minutes of English.
Sticking to a routine makes studying feel easier.

meaning: 최근에, 요즘 — in the recent past; recently
examples: Lately, I've been watching English YouTube videos every morning.
Have you been practising a lot lately?

meaning: 힘들어하다, 애쓰다 / 힘든 과정 — to have difficulty doing something
examples: I used to struggle with pronunciation, but it's getting better.
Learning grammar is a struggle at first, but it gets easier.

meaning: 넓히다, 확장하다 — to make something larger or wider
examples: Reading helps you expand your vocabulary.
I want to expand my speaking skills beyond basic conversation.

meaning: 자신 있는 — certain about your ability; not shy or hesitant
examples: I used to be shy, but now I feel more confident speaking English.
The more you practise, the more confident you become.

meaning: 제안하다 — to put forward an idea for someone to consider
examples: I suggest practising speaking for at least ten minutes a day.
Can you suggest a good way to memorise vocabulary?

meaning: 추천하다, 권하다 — to advise someone that something is good or suitable
examples: I recommend watching English news for fifteen minutes a day.
My teacher recommended using a vocabulary app.

meaning: 격려하다, 권장하다 — to give someone confidence or support to do something
examples: My friend encouraged me to speak even when I make mistakes.
It helps to encourage yourself after each practice session.

meaning: 상기시키다, 떠올리게 하다 — to cause someone to remember something
examples: Please remind me to review those new words tonight.
This grammar rule reminds me of a similar one in Korean.

meaning: 극복하다 — to successfully deal with a difficulty or fear
examples: The best way to overcome a fear of speaking is to speak more.
It takes time to overcome bad habits.

meaning: 이루다, 해내다 — to successfully complete or achieve something
examples: I've accomplished more in a month of daily practice than in a year of sporadic study.
What have you accomplished so far in your language learning journey?

meaning: 성취하다, 달성하다 — to reach a goal through effort
examples: I've finally achieved something I've wanted for years — I held a full conversation in English.
Hard work is the only way to achieve real fluency.

meaning: 진전, 발전 / 발전하다 — forward movement toward a goal
examples: I've made a lot of progress with listening this month.
It's hard to notice progress day by day, but looking back, I've come a long way.

meaning: ~을 따라가다, 유지하다 — to maintain the same pace or level as someone or something
examples: I've been trying to keep up with the news in English every morning.
It's hard to keep up with native speakers at first, but you get used to it.

meaning: (이해가) 딱 맞아 떨어지다, 갑자기 깨달아지다 — when something suddenly becomes clear or makes sense
examples: I've studied grammar rules for months, but yesterday it finally clicked.
Have you ever had that moment when everything just clicks?

meaning: (시간·돈의) 여유가 있다 — to have enough money, time, or ability to do something
examples: If I could afford it, I would take an English course abroad.
I can't afford to miss any more practice sessions this week.

meaning: 몰입하다, 완전히 빠져들다 — to involve yourself deeply in something
examples: If I lived in an English-speaking country, I would immerse myself in the language.
The best way to learn is to immerse yourself completely.

meaning: 해외에서, 외국에 — in or to a foreign country
examples: If I had the chance, I would study abroad for a year.
She lived abroad for three years and her English improved a lot.

meaning: 전념하다, ~에 쏟아붓다 — to give a large amount of time or effort to something
examples: If I had fewer responsibilities, I would dedicate an hour a day to speaking practice.
You need to dedicate real time to it if you want to improve quickly.

meaning: (언어·기술을) 자연스럽게 터득하다 — to learn something informally, without formal study
examples: If I watched English TV every day, I would pick up the accent naturally.
Children pick up languages so quickly because they're not afraid to make mistakes.

meaning: ~할 작정이다, ~할 의도이다 — to have a plan or purpose to do something
examples: I intend to pass the English test by the end of the year.
She didn't intend to be rude — she was just being direct.

meaning: 준비하다, 마련하다 — to make plans or organise something in advance
examples: I've arranged to meet a language exchange partner next Saturday.
Can we arrange a time to practise speaking together?

meaning: ~을 기대하다 — to feel excited about something that will happen (always followed by a noun or -ing, never a base verb)
examples: I'm really looking forward to our next session.
She's looking forward to starting her new job abroad.

meaning: 분명히, 반드시 — without any doubt; used to agree strongly or emphasise certainty
examples: I'm definitely going to review my notes before the next session.
'Are you going to practise speaking today?' 'Definitely!'

meaning: 다가오는, 곧 있을 — happening or expected in the near future
examples: I'm preparing for an upcoming job interview in English.
Do you have any upcoming trips planned?

meaning: ~에도 불구하고 — used to say something happened even though it might have been prevented (always followed by a noun or -ing, NOT a subject+verb clause)
examples: Despite making many mistakes, I kept on speaking.
She passed the exam despite not studying much.

meaning: 가끔, 이따금 — sometimes, but not very often or regularly
examples: I occasionally watch English movies without subtitles.
She occasionally forgets words, but she never stops talking.

meaning: 반면에 — used to contrast two different facts or situations
examples: I find grammar easy, whereas pronunciation is still a challenge.
Some people learn by reading, whereas others prefer listening.

meaning: 상관없이, 그럼에도 불구하고 — without being affected by something else
examples: I'm going to keep practising, regardless of how tired I feel.
Regardless of your level, speaking every day helps.

meaning: 장애물, 걸림돌 — something that makes it difficult to do or achieve something
examples: Fear of making mistakes is the biggest obstacle to speaking naturally.
There will always be obstacles, but you can work through them.

meaning: 언급하다 / 언급 — to say or write something briefly in passing
examples: Did I mention that I've been practising speaking every day?
She mentioned a book that really helped her improve.

meaning: 인상을 주다, 감동시키다 — to make someone feel admiration or respect
examples: I was really impressed by the student who spoke so naturally.
Speaking without hesitation is something that always impresses people.

meaning: 구체적인, 특정한 — relating to one particular thing rather than something general
examples: Can you give me a specific example of what you mean?
I want to work on a specific grammar point that I keep getting wrong.

meaning: 공감하다, ~와 관련이 있다 — to understand and feel connected to something or someone
examples: I can really relate to people who feel nervous about speaking.
Find topics that you can relate to — it makes conversation much easier.

meaning: 충분한, 많은 — a large amount; more than enough
examples: There are plenty of topics that we can talk about in English.
Don't worry — you'll have plenty of chances to practise.

meaning: 보고하다 / 보고 — to give information about something; a piece of information given
examples: She reported that the meeting had gone well.
According to the report, speaking every day really helps.

meaning: 알리다, 통보하다 — to officially tell someone about something
examples: Please inform me if the time changes.
She informed us that the class had been cancelled.

meaning: 주장하다 / 주장 — to say something is true, often without full proof
examples: He claimed that he had practised for ten hours straight.
Her claim that grammar doesn't matter surprised me.

meaning: 부정하다, 부인하다 — to say that something is not true
examples: She denied that she had missed the session.
He denied saying anything rude — he said he was just being direct.

meaning: 인정하다, 시인하다 — to agree that something is true, often reluctantly
examples: I have to admit that speaking is still my weakest skill.
He finally admitted that he hadn't practised at all that week.

meaning: 제조하다, 생산하다 — to make goods in large quantities using machines; the process of making goods
examples: These cars are manufactured in a factory in Busan.
The company manufactures electronics and ships them worldwide.

meaning: 방송하다; 방송 — to send out a programme on TV, radio, or the internet
examples: The game will be broadcast live tonight.
Her speech was broadcast on every major news channel.

meaning: 고용하다 — to give someone a job; to pay them to work for you
examples: She was hired as a customer support specialist.
They hired three new engineers to work on the project.

meaning: 출판하다; 발표하다 — to make a book, article, or piece of information available to the public
examples: His first novel was published when he was only 24.
The results will be published on the official website.

meaning: 교체하다; 대체하다 — to put someone or something new in the place of another
examples: The old manager was replaced by someone from the Seoul office.
My broken laptop was replaced for free under the warranty.
